info.title: Atlas API
info.description: Atlas knowledge and project-management platform REST API

post.summary: Completes account activation in a single DB transaction.
post.description:
Atomicity invariant: the row lock (`FOR UPDATE` on the token SELECT) combined
with the guarded consume (`AND consumed_at IS NULL` on the UPDATE plus a
rows-affected check) guarantee that exactly one activation succeeds per token
even under concurrent POST requests. Under READ COMMITTED, two racers that
reach the SELECT simultaneously will serialize at the lock: the loser blocks
until the winner commits, at which point the loser's `FOR UPDATE` re-read
sees the now-consumed row, the guarded UPDATE matches 0 rows, and the
transaction is rolled back with a 404 — no double-activation, no
double-session.

Steps inside the transaction:
1. Re-validate and lock the token row (`FOR UPDATE`).
2. Consume the token (`AND consumed_at IS NULL`); abort if already consumed.
3. Set the user's `password_hash` and `activated_at`.
4. Create a new session for the newly activated user.

Any failure rolls back the entire transaction: the user stays pending,
the token stays unconsumed, and no session is issued.

get.summary:
Returns every workspace in the system, ordered by creation date.
Restricted to root users via `RequireUserAdmin`.

200.description: Paginated list of the caller's API keys

404.description: Key not found or already revoked

200.description: Grants belonging to this API key

403.description: API keys cannot change a user password

get.summary: Returns the authenticated human user's stored UI state.
get.description:
The state is an opaque JSON object owned by the client. When the user has no
row yet, an empty object `{}` is returned. API keys (agents) are rejected
with 403: agents have no UI.

200.description: The caller's stored UI state, or an empty object

403.description: API keys have no UI state

put.summary: Inserts or replaces the authenticated human user's UI state.
put.description:
The `state` JSON object is stored verbatim; the server does not validate its
inner shape. The stored value is echoed back. API keys (agents) are rejected
with 403: agents have no UI.

200.description: The stored UI state, echoed back

403.description: API keys have no UI state

201.description: Pending user created with activation link

403.description: API keys cannot update a user profile
Not a root/admin user, or target is protected

204.description: Password reset and all sessions revoked

403.description: Not a root/admin user, or target is protected

400.description: Cannot target self or a root user

get.summary:
Returns the workspaces the authenticated principal is a member of.
API keys are workspace-scoped and do not use this endpoint; the result is always empty for them.

post.summary: Creates a new workspace owned by the authenticated human user.
post.description:
The slug is derived from the name and de-duplicated against existing
workspace slugs. The creating user is added as `Owner`, so the workspace
immediately appears in `GET /v1/workspaces`. API keys (agents) are rejected
with 403: agents are workspace-scoped and must not create workspaces.

404.description: Workspace not found or not a member

patch.summary:
Renames the workspace display name. The slug is never re-derived; only
`name` and `updated_at` change. Requires workspace membership.

404.description: Workspace not found or not a member
Workspace not found or principal is not a member

delete.summary: Removes a member from the workspace.
delete.description:
Enforces the same ordered authorization checks as `update_member_role`, adapted for
DELETE:
- Step 3: admin cannot remove an owner.
- Step 4: no one (including break-glass) can remove the last owner → 409.

404.description: Workspace or target member not found

patch.description:
Enforces strictly ordered authorization checks:
1. `WorkspaceOwnerOrAdmin` extractor gates structural access (resolves caller class).
2. Target membership is loaded; 404 if the target is not a member.
3. Role-permission matrix: admins cannot touch owners or promote to owner; others
   are allowed subject to step 4.
4. Last-owner-lockout (independent of step 3, applies to break-glass too): demoting
   the sole remaining owner → 409. This is a data-integrity invariant, not a
   permission — break-glass is NOT exempt.
5. Apply `update_role`. Idempotent: same-role PATCH validates the full matrix first.

Steps 3 and 4 are independent and strictly ordered. Never collapse them.

404.description: Workspace or target member not found

200.description: Caller's saved searches sorted by name (case-insensitive)

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member
Workspace not found or caller is not a member

409.description: A saved search with this name already exists for this owner

422.description: Validation error or per-owner cap exceeded

404.description: Saved search not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member
Saved search not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member

409.description: A saved search with this name already exists for this owner

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query (required). Supports key:value filter tokens.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated kinds: note, task (e.g. note,task). 'all' or empty = no restriction (default). Unknown values are ignored.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor; must match the sort of the issuing request

items.description: A single search result item returned by `GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/search`.

column_name.description: Current column (status) name. Present only when `kind = task`.

project_slug.description: Owning project slug; absent for workspace-root documents with no project.

readable_id.description: Task readable ID (e.g. `"ATL-42"`). Present only when `kind = task`.

snippet.description:
Highlighted snippet with `<mark>…</mark>` markers.
Absent for title-only matches and filter-only queries.

404.description: Workspace not found or principal has no access

422.description: Invalid input: absent q, malformed cursor, or cursor/sort mismatch

200.description: Workspace status templates ordered by position
Distinct label strings used by non-deleted tasks

404.description: Tag not found or not in workspace
Tag not found or not in workspace

409.description: Name already taken by another tag

422.description: Invalid input (blank name or unknown color swatch)

200.description: Caller's task views sorted by name (case-insensitive)

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member
Workspace not found or caller is not a member

409.description: A task view with this name already exists for this owner

422.description: Validation error or per-owner cap exceeded

404.description: Task view not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member
Task view not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member
Task view not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member

409.description: A task view with this name already exists for this owner

parameters.name: column_id
parameters.description: Filter by column id (repeat for multiple)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order: updated_at_desc (default), updated_at_asc, created_at_desc, created_at_asc, priority_desc, title_asc

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member

200.description: Sub-task promoted to a board task

post.summary: Creates a reference from the authorized source task to exactly one target.
post.description:
`task_references` targets are backed by foreign-key columns: the row cannot
be stored without a real target in this workspace. Both-null and both-set
bodies are rejected here as 422 before reaching the DB, preventing a CHECK
constraint violation or a silent both-null insert.

ActivationInfoDto.description:
Minimal user info returned by `GET /v1/activate/{token}` so the activation
page can display a personalised heading without requiring authentication.

ActivationLinkResponse.description:
Response from `POST /v1/users/{user_id}/activation-link`.

`activation_link` is a freshly issued single-use link. Prior unconsumed tokens
for the same user are invalidated.

activation_link.description: Single-use activation path (e.g. `/activate/<token>`). Share this with the invitee.

ActivityEntryDto.description: A single activity entry on a task.

ActorDto.description: Actor attribution attached to revisions and attachments.

secret.description: The full `atlas_`-prefixed secret. Shown exactly once; not stored.

ApiKeyDto.description: Summary representation of an API key (no secret).

ApiKeyGrantDto.description:
A single grant belonging to an API key, with resolved resource labels.

Returned by `GET /v1/api-keys/{key_id}/grants` so the keys panel can display
human-readable resource names without additional lookups.

resource_label.description: Human-readable label: workspace/project name, or id+kind for sub-resources.

workspace_slug.description: Workspace slug (always present — all grants live inside a workspace).

AssigneeDto.description: An actor assigned to a task.

BacklinkDto.description: A document that links to this document (backlink).

BoardSummaryDto.description: Lightweight board summary for list endpoints.

promoted_task_id.description: Set once the item has been promoted to a task.

ColumnDto.description: Column representation (always returned in board context).

ConflictProblemDto.description:
RFC 9457 problem+json extension for CAS revision conflicts (status 409).

Flattens `ProblemDetails` fields alongside conflict-specific fields so the
client receives a single `application/problem+json` body.

base_to_current_patch.description: Unified diff from the client's base revision to the current head.

current_revision_id.description: The revision ID that is currently head (the client's token is stale).

current_seq.description: Sequence number of the current head revision.

CopyDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/documents/{slug}/copy`.

`folder_id` is the destination folder for the copy. When omitted, the copy
lands in the same folder as the source document.

CopyFolderRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/folders/{folder_id}/copy`.

`parent_folder_id` is the destination parent for the copied top folder. When
omitted, the copy lands under the same parent as the source folder.

CreateDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/projects/{ps}/documents`.

The slug is always server-generated; any slug field provided by the client
is silently ignored.

CreateUserApiKeyRequest.description: Request body for `POST /v1/api-keys` (top-level, user-owned key creation).

oneOf.description: Optional initial grant so the key is immediately usable in one workspace.

CreateUserRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/users`.

Creates a pending account with no password. The returned `activation_link`
must be shared with the invitee so they can set their own password.

role.description: Membership role: `"admin"` or `"member"` (owner is rejected with 422).

workspace.description: Workspace slug where the new user will be added.

CreateUserResponse.description:
Response from `POST /v1/users`.

`activation_link` is the plaintext single-use link shown exactly once.
It is not stored — only the hash is kept server-side.

activation_link.description: Single-use activation path (e.g. `/activate/<token>`). Share this with the invitee.

DocumentSummaryDto.description: Lightweight document summary for list endpoints.

FolderDto.description: Folder representation returned by all folder endpoints.

FrontmatterDto.description: Document frontmatter extracted from the leading YAML block.

InitialGrantRequest.description:
Optional initial workspace grant included in a `POST /v1/api-keys` request.

When present, a workspace-scope grant at the given role is created atomically
with the key so the key is immediately usable in that workspace.

role.description: Role: `"viewer"` or `"editor"` (admin is rejected by the agent cap).

workspace.description: Workspace slug for the initial grant.

token.description: Opaque session token — also delivered as an HttpOnly cookie.

parent_folder_id.description: New parent folder ID, or `null` to move to the project root.

items.description: A single activity entry on a task.
Summary representation of an API key (no secret).
A document that links to this document (backlink).
Lightweight board summary for list endpoints.
Lightweight document summary for list endpoints.
Folder representation returned by all folder endpoints.
Lightweight task summary for list endpoints.

assignees.description:
Assigned actors (users and agents), resolved with display names so the
kanban card can render an avatar and the agent badge without a follow-up
request.

board_name.description: Name of the board this task belongs to.

column_name.description: Name of the column (status) this task is currently in.

estimate.description:
Non-negative work estimate in story-point units; surfaced inline (e.g. on a
sub-task row) without loading the full task.

PrincipalDto.description:
A principal (workspace member or agent) that a grant can be addressed to.

Returned by `GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/members` so the share dialog can resolve
a human-readable name to the principal id required by a grant request.

display.description: Display name: the user's display name, or the api key's name.

principal_type.description: `"user"` for a workspace member, `"api_key"` for an agent.

role.description:
Workspace membership role (`"owner"` | `"admin"` | `"member"`).
Present for `user` principals; absent for `api_key` principals.

ProblemDetails.description: RFC 9457 problem+json representation, extended with `request_id` and `hint`.

hint.description: Actionable next-step hint for the caller.

instance.description: Request path — filled by the problem-stamp middleware.

request_id.description: Filled by the problem-stamp middleware from the `x-request-id` header.

title.description: Human-readable summary of the error class.

type.description: Stable error type URI in the form `urn:atlas:error:{slug}`.

board_id.description: Target board for the new task.

column_id.description: Target column for the new task.

ReferenceDto.description: A typed outbound reference from a task to another task or document.

target_resolved.description: False when the target no longer exists (broken ref), consistent with E04.

target_title.description:
Title of the target document, for display. Present only for resolved
document references (task references use `target_readable_id`).

RevisionContentDto.description: Full revision content at a specific sequence number.

RevisionMetaDto.description: Revision metadata returned by the history endpoint.

SavedSearchDto.description: Saved search representation. Does not expose the owner principal.

SearchHitDto.description: A single search result item returned by `GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/search`.

column_name.description: Current column (status) name. Present only when `kind = task`.

project_slug.description: Owning project slug; absent for workspace-root documents with no project.

readable_id.description: Task readable ID (e.g. `"ATL-42"`). Present only when `kind = task`.

snippet.description:
Highlighted snippet with `<mark>…</mark>` markers.
Absent for title-only matches and filter-only queries.

SearchKindDto.description: Discriminant indicating whether a search hit is a document or a task.

ServerMetaDto.description: Response from `GET /v1/meta`. Server build information for the About screen.

url.description: Public base URL of this server, when configured (`ATLAS_SERVER_URL`).

TaskBacklinkDto.description: An inbound reference — another task that points to this one.

board_name.description: Name of the board this task belongs to.

column_name.description: Name of the column (status) this task is currently in.

parent_task_id.description: Set when this task is a sub-task of another; absent for top-level tasks.

TaskPropertiesDto.description: Typed task properties validated at the API boundary.

custom.description: Free-form JSONB escape hatch; no workspace-schema validation in M1.

estimate.description: Non-negative work estimate in story-point units.

TaskSummaryDto.description: Lightweight task summary for list endpoints.

assignees.description:
Assigned actors (users and agents), resolved with display names so the
kanban card can render an avatar and the agent badge without a follow-up
request.

board_name.description: Name of the board this task belongs to.

column_name.description: Name of the column (status) this task is currently in.

estimate.description:
Non-negative work estimate in story-point units; surfaced inline (e.g. on a
sub-task row) without loading the full task.

TaskViewDto.description: Task view representation. Does not expose the owner principal.

TaskViewFiltersDto.description:
Task view filter set as exposed at the API boundary.

All fields are optional. An empty `{}` is a valid "all workspace tasks" view.

assignee.description: Assignee filter. Encoded as a string: "me", "user:{uuid}", "api_key:{uuid}".

column_ids.description: Restrict to these board column ids.

labels.description: Restrict to tasks carrying ALL of these labels.

priorities.description: Restrict to these priority levels. Valid values: low, medium, high, urgent.

sort.description:
Sort order. Valid values: updated_at_desc, updated_at_asc, created_at_desc,
created_at_asc, priority_desc, title_asc.

UiStateDto.description:
Response from `GET /v1/me/ui-state` and `PUT /v1/me/ui-state`.

`state` is an opaque JSON object owned by the client (e.g. which sidebar
folders are collapsed). The server stores and returns it verbatim and does
not validate its inner shape.

UpdateColumnRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /v1/workspaces/{ws}/boards/{board_id}/columns/{column_id}`.

Color uses the same `present_value` convention as `UpdateTaskRequest` fields:
an absent `color` key leaves the color unchanged; explicit `null` clears it;
a string value sets it. `name`, `before`, and `after` remain simple `Option<String>`.

UpdateContentRequest.description:
Request body for `PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/documents/{slug}/content`.

Uses compare-and-swap semantics: `base_revision_id` must match the
document's current revision or the server responds with 409 and a
`ConflictProblemDto` body.

UpdateDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /v1/workspaces/{ws}/documents/{slug}`.

Updates document metadata (title, folder). Use `PUT .../content` to update
content with CAS.

UpdateMeRequest.description: Request body for `PATCH /v1/users/me`. Only the provided fields are updated.

UpdateStatusTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /v1/workspaces/{ws}/status-templates/{id}`.

`name`: `None` = leave unchanged; `Some(v)` = rename.
`color`: absent = leave unchanged; explicit `null` = clear; string = set.
`before`/`after`: position anchors for reorder.

UpdateTagRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /v1/workspaces/{ws}/tags/{tag_id}`.

Both fields are optional: supply `name` to rename, `color` to recolor, or both.
`color: None` in the JSON payload is treated as "leave unchanged"; there is no
way to explicitly clear a tag color via this endpoint (NULL = unset).

UpdateUiStateRequest.description: Request body for `PUT /v1/me/ui-state`. The `state` is stored verbatim.

activated_at.description: `None` means the account has not yet been activated.
